assertions.title: Column Sorting — Ascending/Descending

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:8050 and check Overall column header for sort indicator
steps.expected: Visual sort indicator (arrow) visible on initial load showing descending sort (default)
steps.observed: ALL sort indicator icons have class 'ag-hidden' with display:none. No visual indicator on initial load despite data being sorted descending by overall_score.

steps.expected: Sort toggles to ascending with visible ascending indicator
steps.observed: Descending indicator appeared (ag-sort-descending-icon unhidden). Data remains descending (0.6744, 0.6635, 0.6573...).

steps.action: Click Overall column header second time
steps.expected: Sort toggles to descending with visible descending indicator
steps.observed: ALL sort indicators hidden again. No indicator visible. Data still descending.

steps.action: Click Overall column header third time
steps.observed: Ascending indicator appeared (ag-sort-ascending-icon unhidden). Data now ascending with N/A values at top.

assertions.issues: Two failures: (1) No visual sort indicator on initial load even though data is sorted by overall_score descending via defaultSort config. All ag-sort-indicator-icon elements have 'ag-hidden' class with display:none. (2) 3-state sort cycling (desc→none→asc) instead of required 2-state (asc/desc toggle). The 'none' state in the middle means every other click shows no indicator at all.

assertions.title: Status Display — Distinct Visual Indicators

steps.action: Inspect Status column cells in the AG Grid table
steps.expected: Status values render with unique visual treatment (colored badges)
steps.observed: All status cells contain plain text inside with no styling, no badges, no background colors, no CSS classes differentiating statuses. HTML is just plain text: 'completed'.

steps.action: Compare multiple status values (completed, improved, not_improved, accepted_cv)
steps.expected: Each status visually distinguishable at a glance
steps.observed: All statuses render identically as plain unstyled text. No visual differentiation whatsoever.

assertions.issues: AG Grid cellStyle callback not executing or not configured. Status values are rendered as plain text with no CSS classes or inline styles. The cellRenderer function that should add status-specific styling/badges appears to not be working. The sidebar filter controls DO render colored indicator squares next to status names, but the table cells do not.

assertions.title: Status Color Coding Consistent

steps.action: Compare status badge colors in table vs sidebar status filter
steps.expected: Status colors consistent between table badges and filter controls
steps.observed: Sidebar filter controls have colored indicator squares (e.g., completed=green background rgb(212,237,218), validation_failed=red rgb(248,215,218), baseline=blue rgb(204,229,255)). Table status cells have NO styling at all — plain text with default colors.

steps.action: Check if colors persist after filter/sort operations
steps.observed: Not applicable — no colors exist in table cells to preserve.

assertions.issues: Cannot be consistent because table cells have NO styling. The color definitions exist in the sidebar (Dash HTML components with inline styles), but the AG Grid cellStyle/cellRenderer functions are not applying equivalent styling to table cells. The gap is between Dash-rendered sidebar and AG Grid cell rendering.

assertions.title: Detail View Back Navigation

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:8050, click exp_lme_0098 row to open detail view
steps.observed: Detail overlay opens, URL changes to http://localhost:8050/?experiment=exp_lme_0098, '← Back to Leaderboard' button visible.

steps.expected: Returns to leaderboard view, overlay closes
steps.observed: URL reverts to http://localhost:8050/, but the detail overlay REMAINS visible. '← Back to Leaderboard' button is still displayed. The overlay did not close.

assertions.issues: Browser back button navigates the URL but does not close the detail overlay. The same-page overlay mechanism (dcc.Store-based, not route-based) means history.back() changes the URL but doesn't trigger the close callback. The URL and the view state become desynchronized. Note: the in-app '← Back to Leaderboard' button works correctly.

assertions.title: Best Experiment Highlighted

steps.action: Find exp_lme_0008 row in the leaderboard table
steps.expected: Row visually distinguished (highlighted background, badge, or star)
steps.observed: Row has class 'ag-row-even ag-row-no-focus ag-row ag-row-level-0 ag-row-position-absolute ag-row-not-inline-editing' — standard AG Grid row classes only. No 'best-experiment' class, no highlight class, no visual distinction. Computed background color is rgb(255,255,255) — same as all other even rows.

steps.action: Compare exp_lme_0008 row styling with other rows
steps.observed: No visual difference. Row looks identical to every other row. The rowClassRules configuration that should apply 'best-experiment' class is not executing.

assertions.issues: The .best-experiment CSS class is not applied to the exp_lme_0008 row. AG Grid's rowClassRules function callback is not executing or not matching correctly. The row has only standard AG Grid classes. The sidebar text does mention 'Best: exp_lme_0008' with gold color, but the table row itself has no highlight.

assertions.title: URL State Management

steps.expected: URL updates to include era parameter (e.g., ?era=LongMemEval)
steps.observed: URL remains http://localhost:8050/ — no query params added. The era change is only reflected in the UI button text, not in the URL.

steps.expected: URL preserved, state restored from query params
steps.observed: dcc.Location strips the query params — URL becomes http://localhost:8050/ immediately after page load. However, the state IS restored correctly (era=LongMemEval, detail view open).

steps.action: Click experiment row to open detail view
steps.expected: URL updates to include experiment parameter
steps.observed: URL does update to include experiment parameter (e.g., ?experiment=exp_lme_0098), but era parameter is not included when era is not 'All'.

assertions.issues: Three sub-failures: (1) Era changes do not update the URL — only experiment selection triggers URL update (the update_url_state callback only has selected-experiment as Input). (2) dcc.Location strips query params on page load/refresh, so navigating to a URL with params causes them to be immediately removed (though state is restored internally via restore_from_url callback). (3) Combined era+experiment URL state is incomplete.

summary: Re-tested all 6 previously failing assertions. All 6 remain failing with identical failure modes. No code fixes have been applied since round 1. VAL-TABLE-005: no sort indicator on initial load + 3-state cycling. VAL-TABLE-007: status cells are plain text with no visual styling. VAL-TABLE-008: table cells have no colors to be consistent with. VAL-TABLE-010: browser back changes URL but doesn't close detail overlay. VAL-TABLE-019: best-experiment row class not applied. VAL-TABLE-021: era changes don't update URL, dcc.Location strips query params.
